title: China’s Pursuit of Defense Technologies: Implications for U.S. and Multilateral Export Control and Investment Screening Regimes
description: The hearing assesses China's motivations and policies for defense modernization, including overviews of its military procurement process and military-civil fusion strategy; evaluates how China is pursuing new materials, components, and technologies to address longstanding obstacles in domains such as space, aviation, and undersea warfare, as well as to gain supremacy in new domains such as artificial intelligence; and provides a forward-looking assessment of how Congress, the Administration, and U.S. allies and partners could improve export controls and investment screening to control technology flows to China for use in advanced weapons.
text:
AGENDA
Hearing on "China’s Pursuit of Defense Technologies: Implications for U.S. and Multilateral Export Control and Investment Screening Regimes"
Thursday, April 13, 2023
Dirksen Senate Office Building, Room 419, and Webex
Unable to see this video? Click here.
Hearing Co-Chairs: Chairman Carolyn Bartholomew and Vice Chairman Alex Wong
9:30 AM – 9:40 AM: Co-Chairs' Opening Remarks
9:40 AM – 11:10 AM: Panel I: China’s Defense Modernization Objectives and R&D Ecosystem
Tai Ming Cheung, Professor at University of California, San Diego and Director of the UC Institute on Global Conflict and Cooperation [Testimony]
Christian Curriden, Defense Analyst, RAND Corporation [Testimony]
Elsa Kania, Adjunct Senior Fellow, Technology and National Security Program at the Center for a New American Security [Testimony]
11:10 AM – 11:20 AM: Break
11:20 AM – 1:05 PM: Panel II: Obstacles and Breakthroughs in China’s Defense Technological Development
Kevin Pollpeter, Senior Research Scientist at the Center for Naval Analysis [Testimony]
Chad Ohlandt, Senior Engineer, RAND Corporation [Testimony]
Sarah Kirchberger, Head of Asia-Pacific Strategy and Security at the Institute for Security Policy, Kiel University [Testimony]
Gregory Allen, Director, Wadhwani Center for AI and Advanced Technologies, Center for Strategic and International Studies [Testimony]
1:05 PM – 2:05 PM: Lunch Break
2:05 PM – 3:35 PM: Panel III: Policy Tools for the United States and Its Allies and Partners
Cordell Hull, Visiting Fellow, National Security Institute [Testimony]
Martijn Rasser, Managing Director, Datenna Inc. [Testimony]
Emily Kilcrease, Senior Fellow and Director, Energy, Economics and Security Program at the Center for a New American Security [Testimony]
3:35 PM – 3:40 PM: Closing Remarks
